content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN. You have to count the number of distinct tuples (A,B,C,D)(A, B, C, D)(A,B,C,D) with 1â‰¤A,B,C,Dâ‰¤N1 \leq A, B, C, D \leq N1â‰¤A,B,C,Dâ‰¤N such that Aâ‹…B=Câ‹…DA \cdot B = C \cdot DAâ‹…B=Câ‹…D.

Two tuples, say (A,B,C,D)(A, B, C, D)(A,B,C,D) and (E,F,G,H)(E, F, G, H)(E,F,G,H) are considered to be different if **at least** one of the following conditions hold true:


Aâ‰ EA \neq EAî€ =E
Bâ‰ FB \neq FBî€ =F
Câ‰ GC \neq GCî€ =G
Dâ‰ HD \neq HDî€ =H.

Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains a single integer on a new line - NNN, as described in the problem statement


Output Format
For each testcase, output the answer on a new line - the number of tuples as described in the problem statement.

Test case 111:
Following tuples satisfy the given condition:

(1, 1, 1, 1)
(1, 2, 2, 1)
(1, 3, 3, 1)
(2, 1, 1, 2)
(2, 2, 2, 2)
(2, 3, 3, 2)
(3, 1, 1, 3)
(3, 2, 2, 3)
(3, 3, 3, 3)
(1, 2, 1, 2)
(2, 1, 2, 1)
(1, 3, 1, 3)
(3, 1, 3, 1)
(2, 3, 2, 3)
(3, 2, 3, 2)

Aâ‹…B=Câ‹…DA \cdot B = C \cdot DAâ‹…B=Câ‹…D holds true for all these tuples.
